# Software Developer Survey — TimeHuddle Market Research

> Copy these questions into a Google Form. Each `---` is a new section break.

**Form title**: How Do You Track and Communicate Your Development Work?
**Form description**: We're building a tool for software development teams and
want to understand how developers track their time, communicate progress, and
experience daily rituals like standups and sprint planning. This takes about
6–8 minutes. No right or wrong answers — we want honest impressions of what's
working and what isn't.

---

## Section 1: About You

**Q1 — What best describes your role?**
*Multiple choice*
- Full stack developer
- Frontend developer
- Backend developer
- Mobile developer
- DevOps / platform engineer
- Engineering lead (still hands-on coding)
- Other: ___

**Q2 — How large is your immediate development team?**
*Multiple choice*
- Just me
- 2–5
- 6–15
- More than 15

**Q3 — What's your primary development workflow?**
*Multiple choice*
- Scrum (sprints, standups, planning)
- Kanban (continuous flow, no sprints)
- Hybrid / our own version
- No formal process

---

## Section 2: Tracking Work and Time

**Q4 — Do you track time spent on individual tasks or tickets?**
*Multiple choice*
- Yes — required by my employer
- Yes — I do it for my own awareness
- Sometimes, but inconsistently
- No — I don't track time at that level

**Q5 — When you're deep in a coding session, what typically causes you to
context-switch away from it?**
*Multiple choice — pick all that apply*
- Standup / meeting
- Slack / Teams messages
- Code review requests
- A new urgent bug or ticket
- My own decision to switch
- Other: ___

**Q6 — How do you currently associate time spent with the ticket or PR you were
working on?**
*Long text — open ended (e.g. Jira time logging, nothing, git commits as
proxy)*

**Q7 — How accurately do you feel your logged time or estimates reflect the
actual effort you spent?**
*Scale: 1 (very inaccurate — it's just a guess) → 5 (very accurate)*

---

## Section 3: Standups and Communication

**Q8 — What do you find most useful about your current standup format?**
*Long text — open ended*

**Q9 — What do you find least useful or most annoying about standups?**
*Long text — open ended*

**Q10 — How often do you go into a standup and realize you're not sure what you
actually did yesterday?**
*Multiple choice*
- Almost every day
- Sometimes (once or twice a week)
- Rarely
- Never — I always know

**Q11 — If your standup tool could show you: "Yesterday you worked on these
tickets for these amounts of time, and touched these PRs" — so you just had to
review and summarize — would that help?**
*Scale: 1 (not at all) → 5 (extremely helpful)*

---

## Section 4: GitHub / PR Integration

**Q12 — Do you use GitHub (or GitLab / Bitbucket) for code review and PR
tracking?**
*Multiple choice*
- Yes — GitHub
- Yes — GitLab
- Yes — Bitbucket
- Yes — Azure DevOps
- Other: ___
- No

**Q13 — Are your tickets or work items linked to your PRs or commits today?**
*Multiple choice*
- Yes — automatically (e.g. Jira smart commits, Linear autolinks)
- Yes — manually (I paste links)
- Partially — sometimes linked, sometimes not
- No — they live in separate systems with no connection

**Q14 — If time tracking was connected to your GitHub PR activity — so opening,
reviewing, and merging PRs automatically contributed to time attribution — would
you want that?**
*Multiple choice*
- Yes — that would be more accurate than manual tracking
- Maybe — depends on how it handles context switching
- Not sure
- No — I prefer to control what gets logged
- I don't use GitHub / not applicable

---

## Section 5: Tooling and Friction

**Q15 — What time tracking or project management tools does your team currently
use?**
*Long text — open ended (e.g. Jira, Linear, Harvest, Toggl, nothing)*

**Q16 — What's the single biggest friction point in your current development
workflow that a better tool could realistically fix?**
*Long text — open ended*

**Q17 — How much does tool-switching friction affect your productivity? (e.g.
jumping between Jira, Slack, GitHub, time tracker)**
*Scale: 1 (doesn't bother me at all) → 5 (it significantly impacts my focus
and productivity)*

---

## Section 6: AI and Automation

**Q18 — Are you currently using any AI coding assistants? (GitHub Copilot,
Cursor, etc.)**
*Multiple choice*
- Yes — daily
- Yes — occasionally
- No — but I'm interested
- No — I prefer not to use AI in my coding workflow

**Q19 — Beyond code completion, where would AI assistance in your development
workflow be most valuable?**
*Multiple choice — pick up to 3*
- Drafting standup updates based on my actual activity
- Summarizing what a PR changed in plain English
- Estimating how long a ticket will take based on similar past work
- Flagging when I'm context-switching too much
- Identifying tickets that are taking much longer than estimated
- Generating a sprint summary for stakeholders
- Other: ___

**Q20 — What would make you distrust AI involvement in your work communication
or time tracking?**
*Long text — open ended*

---

## Section 7: Anything Else

**Q21 — What feature or tool do you wish existed that would make your
development workflow significantly better?**
*Long text — open ended*

**Q22 — Any other thoughts you'd like to share?**
*Long text — optional*
